name: Zosteropidae
description: White-eyes are a large family of small passerine birds found across Africa, Asia, and Australasia. They are named for the distinctive white eye-ring that most species possess and are highly active, social birds.

children.name: Zosterops
children.description: The largest genus of white-eyes, with over 100 species found across the Old World tropics.

children.name: Zosterops japonicus
children.description: The Japanese White-eye is a small olive-green bird with a prominent white eye-ring and yellowish throat. It is common throughout East Asia and has become invasive in Hawaii.

children.name: Zosterops lateralis
children.description: The Silvereye is a small, olive-green white-eye with a grey back and a distinctive white eye-ring. It is one of Australia's most common garden birds and also occurs in New Zealand and the southwest Pacific.

children.name: Zosterops virens
children.description: The Cape White-eye is a small, bright olive-green bird with a yellow throat and a bold white eye-ring. It is common in gardens and forests across southern Africa.

children.name: Zosterops senegalensis
children.description: The African Yellow White-eye is a small, bright yellow-olive bird with a conspicuous white eye-ring. It is widespread across sub-Saharan Africa in woodlands and savannas.

children.name: Zosterops erythropleurus
children.description: The Chestnut-flanked White-eye has olive upperparts, white underparts, and distinctive chestnut flanks. It breeds in temperate forests of East Asia and migrates to Southeast Asia for winter.
